UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores profile information for users interacting with the AI Coach Connect app. Authentication data (e.g., passwords) is managed by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication and identification purposes.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

CoachingSession.title: CoachingSession
CoachingSession.description: Represents a single, real-time coaching session between a user and the Simli AI coach, initiated via the 'Start Coaching' button.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CoachingSession entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated this coaching session. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N CoachingSession)

startTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the coaching session began.

endTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the coaching session concluded. This property will be present once the session is completed or terminated.

durationSeconds.description: The total duration of the coaching session in seconds, calculated after the session ends.

status.description: Current status of the coaching session (e.g., 'active', 'completed', 'cancelled', 'failed').

definition.description: Stores individual user profile data. Access is strictly restricted to the user whose authenticated UID matches the `{userId}` in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores details of individual coaching sessions belonging to a specific user. Each document includes a denormalized `userId` field to ensure Authorization Independence, allowing rules to verify ownership without parent document lookups. Access is restricted to the user whose authenticated UID matches the `{userId}` in the path and the `userId` field within the document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this coaching session, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific coaching session.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and QAPs by leveraging path-based ownership and explicit denormalization. User profiles are stored in `/users/{userId}`, making access trivial: only the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`) can read or write their own profile. Coaching sessions are placed in a subcollection, `/users/{userId}/coachingSessions/{sessionId}`.

Authorization Independence for CoachingSession is achieved by including the `userId` directly within each `CoachingSession` document. This means that security rules evaluating access to a specific coaching session can check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without needing to perform a `get()` operation on the parent `/users/{userId}` document. This eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies, enabling atomic operations and simplifying rule debugging.

QAPs (Query as a Principal) are supported for coaching sessions through this hierarchical structure. When a user queries `/users/{userId}/coachingSessions`, Firestore's security rules can implicitly ensure that the `{userId}` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`. This allows for secure `list` operations where users can only retrieve their own coaching sessions, preventing unauthorized data exposure and ensuring rules act as true validators, not filters. The explicit `userId` field within the `CoachingSession` document further reinforces document-level ownership checks during individual `get` or `update` operations.
